title: Method and system for detecting a functional failure in a power gearbox and a gas turbo engine
abstract: The invention relates to a method and system for detecting a functional failure in a power gearbox, in particular an epicyclic gearbox (30) in an aircraft gas turbine engine (10) comprising â¢ a) measuring operational data (101) in the gas turbine engine, â¢ b) obtaining analyzed operational data (102), â¢ c) using the analyzed operational data (102) in a comparison with stored baseline operational data (103) to determine deviation data (104) from the baseline operational data (103), â¢ d) determining time dependent trend data (105) from the deviation data (104) or determining at least a first state measured dependent on the power generation, the power consumption or the power regulation of the gas turbine engine (10) and measuring at least a second state measured dependent on vibrational data of the gas turbine engine (10), and â¢ e) generating a signal and / or a protocol (106) for controlling the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30) and / or the gas turbine engine (10) based on the time dependent trend data (105), in particular if a predetermined condition or threshold is exceeded or based on the at least one first state and the at least one second state.
claims:
Claims Patent claims 1. Method for detecting a functional failure in a power gearbox, in particular an epicyclic gearbox (30) in an aircraft gas turbine engine (10) comprising a) measuring operational data (101) in the gas turbine engine (10) of least one, in particular at least two operational parameters dependent on the power generation and power consumption of the gas turbine engine (10) and / or the power gearbox (30), b) obtaining analyzed operational data (102) comprising time data, angular data of a rotation, frequency data and / or phase data from the measured operational data (102), c) using the analyzed operational data (102) in a comparison with stored baseline operational data (103) to determine deviation data (104) from the baseline operational data (103), d) determining time dependent trend data (105) from the deviation data (104) and / or determining at least a first state measured dependent on the power generation, the power consumption or the power regulation of the gas turbine engine (10) and measuring at least a second state measured dependent on vibrational data of the gas turbine engine (10), and e) generating a signal and / or a protocol (106) for controlling the epicyclic power gearbox (30) and / or the gas turbine engine (10) based on the time dependent trend data (105) or based on the at least one first state and the at least one second state, in particular if a predetermined condition or threshold is exceeded. 2. Method according to claim 1, wherein at least one measured operational parameter (102) and / or at least one baseline operational parameter (103) is â¢ a torque at a shaft (26, 27, 42), â¢ a torque at the power gearbox, epicyclic gearbox (30), 

 â¢ a tangential torque at the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30), â¢ a rotational speed of the shaft (26, 27, 42), â¢ a rotational speed at the input and / or output side of the power gearbox, in particular of the epicyclic gearbox (30), â¢ a power loss over the input and output side of the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30), â¢ speed of the aircraft, â¢ vibrational data at the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30), â¢ fuel intake of the gas turbine engine (10), â¢ a temperature, in particular in the core of the gas turbine engine (10) and / or at the exit of a combustion chamber (16), â¢ a temperature in the feed and / or scavenge oil temperature of the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30), â¢ a position and / or a movement of a variable guide vane in the gas turbine engine (10), â¢ a pressure in the gas turbine engine (10), â¢ a deviation from the nominal in at least one of the above parameters. 3. Method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the time data (102) obtained from the measured operational data (101) is subjected to windowing (203) in signal processing unit (220). 4. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the deviation data (104) comprises a change in magnitude of an amplitude and / or a change in the phase. 5. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the time dependent deviation data (104) comprises data on â¢ at least one rotational speed of a shaft (26,42), â¢ at least one radial, axial and / or tangential dynamic load indicator, â¢ at least one radial, axial and / or tangential vibration. 6. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the trend data (105a, 105b, 

105c) is checked if a condition or threshold (300) is exceed for at least â¢ a trend in a radial, axial and / or tangential vibration, â¢ a trend in a radial, axial and / or tangential dynamic load indicators, â¢ a trend in speed, in particular speed fluctuations. 7. Method of claim 6, wherein the threshold (300) is adapted for range of operation points by the use of a priori knowledge, in particular that known magnitudes of harmonics which are not related to a failure are considered in filtering out relevant harmonics for the failure. 8. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the signal and / or the protocol (106) is used for indicating a functional failure of a bearing, in particular a ball bearing or a journal bearing in the power gearbox, in particular in the epicyclic gearbox (30), the ring gear (38), planet carrier (34), the sun gear (28) and / or the planet gears (32). 9. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the signal and / or the protocol (106) is used for generating a lifetime prediction and / or a maintenance schedule for the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30) and / or the gas turbine engine (10). 10. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein signal and / or protocol (106) is triggered when the measured operational data (101) comprises vibrational frequencies of more than 500 Hz, in particular more than 5000 Hz. 11 . Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the measured operational data (102) is obtained from at least one power sensor, fuel flow sensor, torque sensor, rotational speed sensor, speed sensor, vibration sensor (110), temperature sensor and / or pressure sensor. 12. Method of claim 11 , wherein at least one sensor (110) is positioned at a static part of the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30), in particular at housing of the epicyclic gearbox (30), a ring gear (38) of the epicyclic gearbox (301 and / nr tha 

ring gear mount (41 ) of the epicyclic gearbox (30). 13. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the epicyclic gearbox (30) comprises planetary gears (32) in a star arrangement or in a planetary arrangement. 14. Method of at least one of the preceding claims, with a detection of a vibration signature within a predetermined frequency range, a determination of a property of the vibration signature based on the signals at different points in time and a generation of a command or signal based on a comparison of the property of the vibration signature with a predetermined threshold. 15. System for detecting a functional failure in an power gearbox, in particular an epicyclic gearbox (30) in an aircraft gas turbine engine (10) comprising at least one sensor (110) measuring operational data (101) in the gas turbine engine (10) of least two operational parameters dependent on the power generation and / or power consumption of the gas turbine engine (10) and the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30), computing means for obtaining analyzed operational data (102) comprising time data, angular data of a rotation, frequency data and / or phase data from the measured operational data (101 ) and for using the analyzed operational data (102) in a comparison with stored baseline operational data (103) to determine deviation data (104) from the baseline operational data (103), trend computing means for determining time dependent trend data (105) from the deviation data (104) and / or means for determining at least a first state measured dependent on the power generation, the power consumption or the power regulation of the gas turbine engine (10) and measuring at least a second state measured dependent on vibrational data of the gas turbine engine (10), and signal and / or a protocol (106) generation means for controlling the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30) and / or the gas turbine engine (10) based on the time dependent trend in the deviation data or based on the at least one first state and 

the at least one second state. 16. A gas turbine engine (10) for an aircraft comprising: an engine core (11) comprising a turbine (19), a compressor (14), and a core shaft (26) connecting the turbine to the compressor; a fan (23) located upstream of the engine core, the fan comprising a plurality of fan blades; and a power gearbox, in particular an epicyclic gearbox (30) that receives an input from the core shaft (26) and outputs drive to the fan (23) so as to drive the fan (23) at a lower rotational speed than the core shaft (26), with a system for detecting a failure of the power gearbox, in particular the epicyclic gearbox (30) with the features of claim 15..
